items.title: How and when do Orthodox Christians celebrate Easter?
items.summary: The world's Orthodox Christians will celebrate Easter this weekend, with more than half a million Australians expected to take part.

items.title: Trump says Iran doing 'poor job' of letting oil through Hormuz, shaking fragile truce
items.summary: Iran and the United States said the strait would reopen after the two-week truce was announced on Wednesday.

items.title: Israel agrees to Lebanon peace talks as strike deaths climb above 300
items.summary: The news comes a day after Israel launched its largest wave of strikes on Lebanon since 2 March.

items.title: Global foreign aid in freefall, and Australia is low on the list
items.summary: The OECD says that governments tightening their belts on foreign aid will mean hundreds of thousands of preventable deaths.

items.title: Russia and Ukraine agree to two-day Orthodox Easter ceasefire
items.summary: "People need an Easter without threats and a real move towards peace," Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said.

items.summary: Albanese has warned that a lasting end to the war in the Middle East would not necessarily mean it's "back to business as usual".

items.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic still a trickle despite ceasefire, no new attacks
items.summary: Hundreds of ships with thousands of crew remain stranded on either side of the Strait.

items.summary: An Afghan man whose brother and father were allegedly murdered by Australian soldiers has spoken out following Ben Roberts-Smith's arrest.

items.title: Commute-cutting proposal for Australian workers, as fuel crisis bites
items.summary: Some workers are spending more than $100 extra a week in fuel costs, and there are now calls for more flexible working arrangements.

items.summary: Both sides are already accusing each other of violations. These are the pressure points that could unravel the truce.

items.summary: Residents of far north Queensland are still cleaning up after Tropical Cyclone Narelle carved through the region just weeks ago.

items.summary: Experts say Australia's response to alleged war crimes in Afghanistan has differed from some of its key allies.

items.title: As a 'clog wog', Australians didn't know I was a migrant, so they confided their racism
items.summary: Annette Jorgensen moved here from Denmark aged two. Living as a migrant while looking like a white Australian was an insightful experience.

items.title: South Australian mother faces deportation after passport mistake
items.summary: A South Australian community is rallying around a woman who could be deported, leaving behind her husband and children.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith to remain in custody while charged with war crimes
items.summary: Australia's most decorated war veteran, Ben Roberts-Smith will remain in custody after a brief court mention this morning. The Victoria Cross recipient has been charged with five counts of the war crime of murder. Phoebe Deas reports from Silverwater correctional complex in Sydney.

items.title: Kelvin is a stay-at-home dad, but many young people believe it's not a man's role
items.summary: As traditional views of masculinity regain traction among some young men, Kelvin is challenging ideas around a father's role.

items.title: Trump announces 'double-sided' ceasefire as Iran gives two-week Hormuz passage pledge
items.summary: Iran said it would allow "safe passage" through the Strait of Hormuz, but only in coordination with its armed forces and subject to "technical limitations".

items.title: Why Trump can't walk away from the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The US president's inconsistent messaging about the Strait of Hormuz signals no "clear strategy", experts say.

items.title: Gunfight outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul leaves one attacker dead
items.summary: Two of the attackers were said to be brothers, who had travelled to Istanbul from the city of Izmit.
